key_insight: Eco path integrals optimize the internal ring-polymer frequencies to better match quantum harmonic behavior while keeping the same simple Trotter-style workflow.
engineering_relevance: This gives near-Suzuki-Chin accuracy with much lower implementation overhead, making high-accuracy quantum nuclear sampling easier to deploy in existing codes.
method: Fit the non-zero normal-mode frequencies by least squares to exact quantum radii of gyration.
Keep the standard bead-to-normal-mode transformation and the rest of the Trotter path-integral machinery unchanged.
Use the optimized frequencies to improve convergence for observables in realistic materials simulations.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports that for hexagonal ice the Eco path integral converges comparably to 4th-order Suzuki-Chin while retaining 2nd-order Trotter effort, and it was also applied in GPUMD to MOF-5 thermal expansion and heat capacity.
watch_item: The optimization is tuned against harmonic-ring-polymer radius-of-gyration targets, so performance may depend on the frequency range and problem class used to build the Eco parameters.
